nativeRange.habitat: Shallow reef habitats and rocky intertidal to subtidal zones; typically found attached to hard substrates such as rock, rubble, and dead coral in the photic zone

avoidWith: herbivorous fish likely to graze heavily on macroalgae

imageCandidates.notes: Primary product photo from Mosaic Macros listing for Intricate Laurencia; shows compact branching red thallus with knobbed tips.

summary: Intricate Laurencia (Laurencia intricata) is a compact red macroalgae with densely branching stems tipped with small knobs, available in red, yellow, and pink color forms. An ornamental display species rather than a nutrient exporter, it tends to be more sensitive to shipping stress than most macroalgae and benefits from careful acclimation.
careNotes:
Intricate Laurencia (Laurencia intricata, family Rhodomelaceae, order Ceramiales) is a small, erect-branching red macroalgae with dense, knobbed branch tips. It occurs in red, yellow, and pink color forms, and is typically sold on plugs or as loose fragments of 5 inches or more. It is currently available from a small number of specialty macroalgae retailers rather than through general aquarium trade channels.

This species is VERY sensitive to shipping, to a degree that is unusual even among specialty macroalgae. Expedited air shipping (UPS 2nd-Day or Next-Day Air) is required to qualify for a live arrival guarantee from vendors that offer one, reflecting its sensitivity to temperature fluctuation and physical stress during transit. Careful, patient acclimation on arrival is strongly advisable.

Water parameters consistent with the broader Laurencia genus and family Rhodomelaceae apply: temperature generally around 24–27°C, salinity 1.023–1.026 SG, and pH 7.8–8.4 — standard reef water quality. Moderate (medium-intensity) lighting tends to be recommended for ornamental red macroalgae of this family. Securing the holdfast (the root-like anchoring structure) to live rock or rubble tends to improve long-term success, and good water flow has been reported to benefit health in closely related Laurencia species.

Nutrient uptake is generally considered low for this species, making it better suited as an ornamental display piece than as a refugium nutrient exporter. Propagation in the aquarium is typically achieved by fragmentation. Herbivorous fish and sea urchins may graze on it, so isolation from heavy grazers is generally advisable when keeping it as a display specimen.

As a red alga, Intricate Laurencia will MELT at the base if buried in sand. It should be attached to rock rather than placed on a sand bed — common methods include superglue gel, zip-tie or fishing line, or simply wedging the base into a crevice in live rock. Sand-bed placement is generally better suited to green macroalgae genera such as Caulerpa, Halimeda, Penicillus, Rhipocephalus, and Udotea, or to some calcified Dasycladales.

primary.name: Mosaic Macros
primary.notes: Sole hobby source for this species. Product page confirms Laurencia intricata scientific name, compact knobby branching morphology, red/yellow/pink color forms, and extreme shipping sensitivity requiring expedited air shipping.

additional.name: AquaMate – Color-Shifting Laurencia (sw-algae-030, Yuzurua iridescens)
additional.notes: Sibling Laurencia entry (family Rhodomelaceae, order Ceramiales) used as cross-reference for water parameters, care level, and placement conventions. Species-level researched entry.

additional.name: AquaMate – Flat Purple Laurencia (sw-algae-040, Laurencia sp.)
additional.notes: Genus-level sibling Laurencia consolidation entry used as secondary cross-reference for family-level husbandry parameters.

additional.name: Mosaic Macros general macroalgae care guide
additional.notes: Slice-wide secondary source — provides authoritative Mosaic-wide parameter targets (temp 22-26°C, pH 8.1-8.4, Ca 400-450 ppm, Mg 1250-1350 ppm, NO3 5-20 ppm, PO4 0.02-0.1 ppm) and the 4-method propagation standard. Added retroactively 2026-06-08.
